scope: Reusable decision framework for future high-risk Hyprland scalar rows that may affect display/render, cursor/input, debug/crash, or other recovery-sensitive behavior.
notUniversalWarning: This pattern is a reusable decision framework, not automatic permission to enable rows. Each future high-risk row still requires source-backed, row-specific proof.
requiredEvidenceBeforeWritable: official Hyprland documentation or source evidence for the setting and accepted value shape
Rust validator proof for accepted and rejected values
fixture/temp write, reread, rollback, and gate proof
risk classification tied to the row's actual failure mode
tests proving counts, allowlists, and unrelated rows remain unchanged
advisoryChecksPolicy: Advisory checks may provide additional user information, but they do not replace source proof, validators, recovery/watchdog gates, or production write safety.
uiSeparationPolicy: Advanced advisory UI actions must be separated from write/apply controls and must not approve, block, or bypass writes unless a future approved sprint explicitly makes them part of write safety with tests.
unifiedPipelineReportPolicy: Each high-risk row must link its source proof, validator proof, write safety proof, gate proof, recovery proof, UI warning, and remaining deferred work in aggregate reports.
testsRequired: row remains blocked or writable only according to the approved policy
SAFE_WRITABLE_ROWS length is unchanged unless an approved enablement sprint proves otherwise
write allowlist is unchanged unless explicitly approved
ungated risky writes are rejected when a gate is required
valid gated fixture/temp writes are accepted when enablement is approved
unrelated rows remain on their existing path
real config and active runtime are untouched in tests unless explicitly approved
advisory output cannot approve, block, or bypass writes unless explicitly designed as write safety
doNotInferSafetyFrom: a policy name without source-backed evidence and tests
batchingPolicy: Prefer grouped high-risk bucket readiness and small approved batches over endless one-row deep dives, unless a row has a unique proven risk that requires isolation.
proofUsed: screen shader production gate approval proof
screen shader advisory helper/UI/file chooser execution proof reports
current Rust write safety and UI source
proofStillMissing: row-specific source-backed proof for each future high-risk row
bucket-specific recovery proof before future grouped enablement
approval for any future write allowlist/count change
nextRecommendedSprint: Next high-risk bucket readiness and batching sprint
